pi_items.source: Task description (step 4: 'outline 3 distinct, complete nuclei') and verifier.py (line 67: requires rois >= 3)

ImageJ documentation confirms RGB Stack conversion creates exactly 3 slices (Red, Green, Blue channels). Task uses built-in Fiji 'Fluorescent Cells' RGB sample image converted to stack via 'Image > Type > RGB Stack'
Metadata value only appears in task.json. Not referenced in setup_task.sh, export_result.sh, or verifier.py. The verification script does not check for minimum ROI area constraints.

Environment configuration (env.json line 116) defines user account 'ga'. This path is consistently used in setup_task.sh (line 9: RESULTS_DIR), export_result.sh (line 12: RESULT_FILE), and verifier.py (line 25)

privileged_info_summary: The task requires measuring signal intensity across 3 channels (Red, Green, Blue) from 3 distinct nuclei using the Fiji/ImageJ built-in 'Fluorescent Cells' sample image. When this RGB image is converted to an RGB Stack, it creates exactly 3 slices (one per channel). The verifier checks that: (1) at least 3 ROIs are measured, (2) measurements span all 3 slices/channels, (3) channel variance is detected (indicating actual multi-channel data), and (4) nuclear signal is detected on Slice 3 (Blue/DAPI channel with intensity > 30). Results are saved to /home/ga/ImageJ_Data/results/nuclei_stack_profiles.csv. The metadata claim of minimum ROI area of 500 pixels is not enforced by the verification logic.
